history - Spending limits for token transfers Use Cases: - Test treasury draining attacks - Simulate emergency fund access - Verify defense mechanisms protect treasury - Track unauthorized withdrawal owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

attemptWithdrawal(address,uint256,addres.notice: Attempt to withdraw funds (simulates attack) Tracks where attackers attempt to withdraw from

emergencyPause().notice: Emergency pause function (simulates guardian pause) Prevents all operations on treasury

emergencyRecoverToken(address,uint256,ad.notice: Emergency token recovery (only owner) Used to recover tokens in exceptional cases

executeWithdrawal(uint256).notice: Execute a proposed withdrawal (multi-sig approval required)

failedWithdrawalAttempts(address).notice: Tracking of failed withdrawal attempts (for security analysis)

getBalance(address).notice: Get treasury balance for a token

getConfirmationCount(uint256).notice: Returns current confirmations for a transaction from active signers.

getTokens().notice: Get all tokens held by treasury

getTreasuryComposition().notice: Get total treasury value (in terms of number of different tokens)

multiSigEnabled().notice: Whether to enforce multi-sig or allow single-signer operations

spendingLimit().notice: Maximum amount that can be transferred per transaction without multi-sig approval
